Reports

Merlin includes eight built-in reports under Reports in the navigation. /reports redirects to /reports/income-statement. Reports are split into two groups: ledger-centric financial statements (read from document_lines and documents headers joined through accounts → account_groups → account_types) and operational reports (expense and AI metrics).

Income Statement

Found at /reports/income-statement. Reads income (account type code 4) and expense (type code 5) document lines from posted documents. Shows two columns: month-to-date (start of current month → today) and financial year-to-date (FY start → today). Groups lines by AccountGroup. Net profit/loss is income minus expenses. Data source: document_lines joined to documents/accounts/account_groups/account_types.

Balance Sheet

Found at /reports/balance-sheet. Shows assets (type code 1), liabilities (type code 2), and equity (type code 3) account balances. Includes an accounting-equation validation: assets must equal liabilities plus equity. Any imbalance indicates a data issue. Groups by AccountGroup.

There is no separate ledger/journal table — balances are computed at query time from seven sources: sales invoice lines (credit income), purchase invoice lines (debit expense), sales invoice headers (debit accounts receivable by total), purchase invoice headers (credit accounts payable by total), and payment-type document headers split by direction — inbound payments credit receivable and debit the contra (bank) account, outbound payments debit payable and credit the contra account. Every settlement path (manual payment, bank-statement reconciliation, InvoiceNinja import) must create a payment document with the correct account-id fields for this to balance; mutating an invoice's amount_paid/balance_due alone is invisible to this report.

Client receivable sub-accounts (see Chart of Accounts) roll up into their parent control account for display — a client's own account never appears as a separate balance-sheet line, only the summed control-account total does.

Trial Balance

Found at /reports/trial-balance. Aggregates debit and credit totals across all account types for the selected period. Uses the same seven-source query as the balance sheet, computed twice — once for period movements, once for the cumulative balance — and rolled up the same way. Total debits must equal total credits.

Income by Account

Found at /reports/income-by-account. Ledger-centric: totals income document lines grouped by GL account for the period. Includes both sales invoices and any other income-type postings (e.g. imported InvoiceNinja invoices).

Income by Client

Found at /reports/income-by-client. Totals income grouped by the party on each document for the period. Shows which clients generated the most revenue.

Expenses by Account

Found at /reports/expenses-by-account. Shows total posted purchase invoice spend grouped by GL account over the selected date range. Source: posted purchase invoices only (not the full ledger). Groups by AccountGroup.

Expenses by Supplier

Found at /reports/expenses-by-supplier. Shows total posted purchase invoice spend grouped by supplier (party) over the selected date range.

LLM Performance

Found at /reports/llm-performance. Aggregated metrics from the llm_logs table: token usage totals, confidence score distribution, model tier breakdown (fast/strong/backup), and fallback frequency. Requires the view-llm-summary permission. For individual extraction details see LLM Logs.

Date ranges and the financial year

All reports default to the current financial year. Boundaries are calculated by FinancialYearService using AccountingSettings::financial_year_start_month (Settings › Accounting tab). For example, with a March start, the financial year runs 1 March → last day of February. Each report accepts a custom date range override.

Permissions

Financial statement reports (income statement, balance sheet, trial balance, income by account/client, expenses by account/supplier) require documents-view-any. LLM Performance additionally requires view-llm-summary.
